$comment: Shared golden vectors for the Pages `PathGlob` filter primitive (#1910). The SAME file is asserted by the Rust query path (src-tauri/.../pages_path_glob_conformance_tests.rs, driving the real list_pages_with_metadata_inner with a PathGlob filter through LOWER(title) GLOB ?) and the tauri-mock TS reimplementation (src/lib/search-query/__tests__/glob-conformance.test.ts, driving pageGlobFilterMatches). The backend uses the SQLite GLOB dialect after #1320-A (prepare_globs: top-level comma split -> validate -> bounded brace expansion -> `[`-aware substring wrap -> ASCII-only lowercase, then `LOWER(title) GLOB ?`). If backend semantics change, regenerate this file from the Rust side; the mock test then fails until glob-validate.ts is realigned. Only the SET of matching page ids is compared cross-impl (representation-stable). Scenarios exercise the seven divergence classes from #1910 (brace expansion, char classes/ranges, literal-bracket flip, substring-wrap trigger, validation/rejection, whitespace trimming, ASCII-only folding).

scenarios.name: brace expansion — {foo,bar} unions two substring-wrapped globs
character class — [ab]test matches a/b prefix, not c
char class + ASCII fold — [pP]roj* matches Projects
brace + path — {alpha,beta}/* matches both path prefixes
exclude — not {foo,bar} keeps every other row
bare word substring (preserved) — repo wraps to *repo*
ASCII-only fold — café matches Café (é case preserved both sides)
ASCII-only fold — ascii 'cafe' does NOT match 'Café' (e != é)
whitespace-only — trimmed to nothing, constrains nothing
